hypothesis: If deep models are trained semi-supervised with an ensemble-consensus consistency loss that pulls each member toward the ensemble mean on unlabeled data (with gradient detached), then they will outperform supervised training and existing SSL methods (mean-teacher, PSEUDOσ) in predictive accuracy and calibration on molecular graph and image benchmarks, with larger ensemble sizes producing larger gains that are robust to the choice of coupling weight γ and loss function.
